rulings.Case Title: FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL VS. MICHAEL B. ROTHENBERG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Rothenberg Ventures Llc To Strike The Verified Second Amended Complaint For Breach Of Contract And Fraud Pursuant To Ccp Sec. 438(I)(1)(A) Or, Alternatively, For Judgment On The Amended Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 21, 2022 line 1. NOTICE OF MOTION OF ROTHENBERG VENTURES LLC TO STRIKE THE VERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND FRAUD PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 438(I)(1)(A) OR, ALTERNATIVELY, FOR JUDGMENT ON THE AMENDED PLEADINGS is continued to November 10, 2022 per stipulation and order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ONE GRANT PROPERTY OWNER LLC VS. 1AND8 INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 21, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT 1AND8 INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is DENIED. According to the evidence presented by the moving party, "1and8, Inc, a New York corporation," the signatory to the contract merged into "1and8, Inc, a Delaware corporation," shortly after the contract was executed. "1and8, Inc, a Delaware corporation," the sole defendant in this action was served by a registered process server on its agent for process of service, Rennie Gardiner. Gardiner declaration fails to overcome the presumption afforded registered process servers. "1and8, Inc, a Delaware corporation" fails to meet the standard under CCP 473 or Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HOMERISESF VS. DANA BETINIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 21, 2022 line 11. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. Defendant appeared in this action on December 1, 2021. No authority is cited for the proposition that a Motion or Judgment on the pleadings is properly set on less than 16-court day notice under the circumstances of this case. Authorities cited in the moving papers are factually and procedurally distinguishable. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
